channel.title: SCP Draft: The Subdirective, and variations on ΩK
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "SCP Draft: The Subdirective, and variations on ΩK" - I have an SCP draft with the very original concept of Death dying.

item.title: Re: SCP Draft: The Subdirective, and variations on ΩK
item.content:encoded:
So it turns out that existing SCP I was thinking about is an 001 proposal - http://www.scp-wiki.net/tanhony-s-proposal
I haven't re-read it but after a brief skim of some sections there might be a couple more similarities to yours than I thought, so it's probably worth checking out before you post to see what you think. I'd additionally make sure that you point this out to anyone else you get feedback from to see what they think on the subject.

item.title: Re: SCP Draft: The Subdirective, and variations on ΩK
item.content:encoded: Thanks a ton for the critique, I'll take what you said to heart and update you when it's posted.

item.title: Re: SCP Draft: The Subdirective, and variations on ΩK
item.content:encoded:
Feedback complete. I apologise if this is overly rambley!

Notes
– hide block

Clandestine facility Area-03, located two kilometers beneath Hong Kong city, has been created in order to perpetually contain SCP-XXXX.

I would drop the "Clandestine facility" part here, as that is implied with all Foundation sites, I think, and the word "perpetually" here seems unnecessary. Also, I've never heard to it referred to as "Hong Kong city", and nothing is coming up on google regarding that. I'm not an expert obviously, but maybe double-check that one, or find a more accurate name?

Maintenance of the Strayer Density Matrix is synonymous with SCP-XXXX's containment and requires a specialized team of technicians and speculative ultraphysicalists.

The "synonymous" part reads a little formally to me; I'd say "is integral to SCP-XXXX's containment". I'd also have a footnote next to speculative ultraphysicalists as I have no idea what that means, and your own sandbox is like the fourth result on google for it.

omnipresent entity, having permeated itself through the known universe.

having permeated -> which has permeated. Change this on both versions of the document.

The effect of SCP-XXXX's perpetuated existence

Drop the word "perpetuated" here.

killed in the following, localized CK-class

No need for the comma here

It is unclear what the "Causation" column of your table is referring to. The thing that caused the entity to manifest? I'd give it a different title to make this more clear.

and SCP-XXXX's immediate resuscitation

"and its immediate resuscitation".

mild pulling force

massive pulling force

"pulling" force reads a little loose here, maybe change it to "gravitational force".

You mention a lot in the first document civilians accidentally affected by the termination of SCP-XXXX, but you never actually mention what those effects are as far as I can tell.

Thoughts
– hide block

Ok so, first of all, this is not "short-ish" :p
I'll apologize if my thoughts here seem a little scatter-shot, I wasn't expecting to crit an article this long so I'm kind of piecing my thoughts on it together as I go. I'll go document by document and then try and tie it all together.

Document 1
I initially found the vagueness of this frustrating, especially how unclear the table is - I still don't know what the causation column is meant to be. While I now understand why it's so vague, I feel like you need more than the single line at the end of the containment procedures to preface this fact. I'd consider adding a header banner with something to the effect of "This document is necessarily vague for the purposes of security" to the top of the document.

Additionally, you mention resuscitating SCP-XXXX after its death but none of the injuries mentioned in the table are things you can typically resuscitate a person from. Maybe you need a different word there? Resurrect, maybe?

It's also unclear what happened to the thing that was summoned in 1987 - the first entries in the table, presumably folllowing its manifesation in the second document - start in 1999. So where was it in the interim? What happened to that version that was summoned.

Document 2
There was an amazing "oh shit" moment here as I put together all the vagueness from the first document and worked out what was going on. That worked really well. I'm hit by the word "resuscitation" here again though - and finally piecing it together now, what you want is to describe its essence being transferred into a new form or body. You aren't bringing its dead body back to life, you're moving its very concept into a new shell. I think if you fix that in this and the first docs, the whole thing will work a lot better.

The lecture log is interesting, but I'm not really sure it adds much in its current form. The concept of Intrinsics is interesting but the stinger - that death is dying - is already known from the description. I'll leave this up to you, but I'd consider cutting out as much of this as you feel you can, and playing up the angle that SCP-XXXX manifested itself in order to turn itself in, as this is the big take away from the entire lecture, I feel. It explains the manifestation event before it, the tables from the first document and the entire 3rd document.

Document 3
2 primary issues here. 1 - why would they transfer SCP-XXXX into the body of a largely indestructible death robot that apparently wants to escape, and why is that robot apparently the largely augmented body of the Administrator? The image is cool but I feel like I'm going to need a lot more justifaction before I buy this. The administrator thing you could explain as being one of the conditions SCP-XXXX gave - it did turn itself in to the Foundation after all. But why the giant robot?

Issue 2 is that I don't like the whole Subdirective of the Foundation thing being that it is meant to kill death, especially since when presented with the opportunity to do so, they don't because they realise what a stupid idea it is. If it were me, I'd drop that angle entirely and go with the necessity of preserving death as a concept as the angle.

Summary
– hide block

So, overall, despite my frustrations, I really enjoyed this. That moment during the second document when I realised what was going on was genuinely awesome and made me very impressed with everything from the first. I've obviously left a lot of notes above, which you are free to use as you choose, but I think if you can avoid the frustrations that can easily occur from an article like this, tighten up a couple of places and fix a few logical issues, this could do very well.

My one concern is that I'm fairly sure I've read an article which featured the physical embodiment of death before. I can't find it now, even through googling, so I might be misremembering but I don't think it should be a huge issue regardless. There is no canon, obviously, but with some tweaks this will be more than strong enough to endure those comparisons.

Hopefully this long, rambling mess of a feedback post is helpful to you. I'll try and check in if you want any claifications on anything I've said but feel free to drop me a PM if I don't respond in a timely manner.

item.title: SCP Draft: The Subdirective, and variations on ΩK
item.content:encoded:
Hey, all. I have a new, short-ish SCP article with the very original hook of a certain hooded Reaper dying. I'm concerned that the events of the article are a bit too obscure, and need more overt clarification for the reader.

Here's a link to the sandbox, it's up at the top. I appreciate any feedback I can get.
